dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for a sick person.
Hi, I'm Ryan, an EMT. What made you call 911 today?
He's an older man with altered mental status and weakness. We saw him at the methadone clinic.
What was the main issue when you arrived?
I felt very weak and disoriented since waking up this morning.
Can you tell me your name and where we are?
My name is Carl, and I believe we are in Richmond.
Did you have your eyes open when we arrived?
Yes, he had his eyes open but didn't make eye contact.
Did he respond when we asked questions or tried to assist?
He nodded slightly and could not stand without help.
Airway is patent and self-maintained.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Breathing is regular, 16 breaths per minute.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Pulse is 78, strong and regular.
Skin is normal in color and dry.

dialogue.micro_intent: assessment of motor status - unsteady gait
dialogue.utterance: He has an unsteady gait and requires assistance.
Carl has a GCS of 14: E4, V4, and M6.
Let me perform the BEAST exam. Carl, can you raise both arms?
He attempted to raise both arms, with some drift noted, no focal deficit.
When did you first feel weak?
Around 8:00 a.m., I could barely get moving.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: He was active last night, walking independently.
What medical history do you have?
He has liver cancer and liver disease.
What was your last meal or drink?
I haven't eaten anything in two days due to no appetite.
Respiration remains stable at 16 breaths per minute.
Blood pressure is 87 over 66 mmHg.
Heart rate is 78 beats per minute, regular.
Blood glucose is 37 mg/dL. That's low.
Oxygen saturation is at 95% on room air.
Administration of oral glucose will help raise your blood sugar.
Administering 15 grams of oral glucose. It might taste unpleasant.
Applying a 4-lead monitor for cardiac rhythm assessment.
4-lead ECG in place and showing sinus rhythm.
12-lead ECG performed. Interpretation shows non-specific T wave abnormality.
Following hypoglycemia and AMS protocols due to altered mental status and low glucose.
Retaking glucose after first dose reveals it is 0 - he started eating.
Heart rate is now 75 beats per minute.
Blood pressure is 93 over 61 mmHg.
Respiration rate has increased slightly to 20 breaths per minute.
Oxygen saturation improved to 97%.
He completed about half the glucose quickly. Allow him to proceed.
Glucose now is 39 - still low but improving.
Saturation has slightly dropped to 90%. We'll continue monitoring.
Second ECG shows a non-specific intraventricular conduction delay.

dialogue.micro_intent: ECG findings - nonspecific T wave abnormality
dialogue.utterance: Non-specific T wave changes still present on ECG.

dialogue.micro_intent: central nervous system - mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: Can you tell me the date today?

dialogue.micro_intent: central nervous system - mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: I'm not sure. I think it's the weekend.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how severe is your fatigue?
I'd say 0, but I'm just tired and weak.
Which hospital would you prefer to be taken to?
He prefers local VCU Health.
Let's assist him onto the stretcher slowly. I'll take his right side.
Carefully laid onto the stretcher with assistance from both sides.

dialogue.micro_intent: iv start - extremity vein (arm or leg)
dialogue.utterance: Attempting IV start in left forearm.

dialogue.micro_intent: iv start - extremity vein (arm or leg)
dialogue.utterance: IV attempt unsuccessful due to difficult venous access.
Stabilized on Ferno stretcher with five-point restraint.
He's still AAOx2/4 with improved level of consciousness.
There was a 25-minute delay due to ED bed assignment issues.
Final vitals: blood pressure 84 over 61 mmHg.
Mr. Carl is en route to VCU with oxygen, glucose administered, and no IV started.
Blood glucose has improved to 48 mg/dL after finishing oral glucose in the ED.
No known drug allergies. History includes liver cancer and liver disease.
We'll ensure his sons meet us at the hospital for continuity of care.
Report provided directly to the ER nurse upon arrival.
